summary: Moved Captain's `ftCa_SpecialLw` from an assembly object into `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialLw.c`, partially decompiling the full Falcon Kick action family while retaining inline asm under `MUST_MATCH` for functions that were not yet cleanly matched. The PR also added Captain SpecialLw data/motion-var structure fields, exported the SpecialLw enter prototypes, fixed an include path in `ftCo_Init.h`, converted nearby data literals to typed `.float`/`.double`, and touched tooling used to convert data and report asm-differ scores. The included score report shows all listed functions in the new C file in the roughly 91-99% range, confirming this was a broad partial-match conversion rather than a full match.
what_changed: Replaced `$(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialLw.s.o` with `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialLw.c.o` in `obj_files.mk`, making the new C translation unit the built object.

Renamed/consolidated the old `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialLw.s` into `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialLw.c` with C implementations for many simple animation, physics, and collision callbacks, plus inline asm fallbacks for harder functions.

Implemented or partially implemented routines around `ftCa_SpecialLw_Enter`, `ftCa_SpecialAirLw_Enter`, `ftCa_SpecialLw_Anim`, `ftCa_SpecialAirLw_Anim`, end-state animation callbacks, physics callbacks, and collision callbacks.

Added a C helper `ftCa_SpecialHi_800E400C` that reads `fp->mv.ca.speciallw` and `ftCaptain_DatAttrs`, incrementing `speciallw.x0` and multiplying `speciallw.friction` by `speciallw_on_hit_spd_modifier` while within `speciallw_unk2`.

Added typed constants in the C file for `ftCa_SpecialHi_804D9220`, `ftCa_SpecialHi_804D9224`, `ftCa_SpecialHi_804D9228`, and `ftCa_SpecialHi_804D922C`, with a local TODO noting that they are all literals.
Kept `ftCa_SpecialHi_800E3EAC` as MWERKS inline asm, with a non-MWERKS `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stub, indicating the function remained too sensitive or unresolved for portable C in this PR.
Wrapped several functions with `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` inline asm versions and C versions otherwise, preserving build/matching behavior while documenting candidate C logic.
Added public prototypes for `ftCa_SpecialLw_Enter` and `ftCa_SpecialAirLw_Enter` to `ftCa_SpecialLw.h`.

Expanded `ftCaptain_DatAttrs` comments with byte offsets and clarified SpecialLw fields such as `speciallw_flame_particle_angle`, `speciallw_on_hit_spd_modifier`, `speciallw_ground_lag_mul`, `speciallw_landing_lag_mul`, `speciallw_ground_traction`, and `speciallw_air_landing_traction`.
Added `ftCaptainSpecialLwVars` inside `ftCaptain_MotionVars`, mapping the SpecialLw motion variables at `fp+2340`/`fp+2344` to `x0`, `x2`, and `friction`.
Updated `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialHi.s` data literals from raw `.4byte` encodings to `.float 0.0`, `.float 1.0`, and `.double 1.5707963267948966`.

Updated `tools/convert_data.py` to target `**/ftCaptain/*.s`, modernize formatting, and preserve helpers for converting raw `.4byte` data to `.float`, `.double`, `.int`, `.asciz`, or hex based on filters.
Updated `tools/report_score.py` formatting/import order and subprocess layout while continuing to emit a markdown table of asm-differ scores.
smart_moves: Used a hybrid inline-asm plus C strategy: difficult functions stayed match-preserving under `MUST_MATCH`, while readable C alternatives were added for decompilation progress and WIP builds.
Kept explicit unused-stack padding blocks under `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` in several C versions, a common tactic when local stack shape affects matching.
Introduced `ftCaptain_DatAttrs` offset comments before relying on newly named SpecialLw attributes, reducing risk of silently shifting character attribute layout.
Modeled SpecialLw per-action state through `fp->mv.ca.speciallw` instead of leaving raw offsets like `0x2340` and `0x2344` throughout the new C logic.
Converted raw data words to typed `.float`/`.double` forms, making literal pools easier to audit and reducing cognitive load when matching float/double loads.
Retained callback wiring for hitlag pause/resume through `efLib_PauseAll` and `efLib_ResumeAll`, preserving effect behavior across decompiled enter/transition paths.
Included a function-by-function asm-differ score report in the PR body, making the partial-match status visible and searchable.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter action files, it is useful to migrate the object from asm to C even before every function is fully matched, as long as inline asm guards preserve exact matching where needed.
When action code uses character attribute blocks, add offset-annotated struct fields before replacing raw loads; this keeps later reviewers and matchers aligned on layout assumptions.
Motion-variable unions are the right place to name action-local state such as Falcon Kick hit counters/friction, avoiding repeated raw `fp+2340` style reasoning.
Simple callbacks that only check `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining` and transition to common routines are good early wins during partial decompilation.
Preserve known callback side effects, especially hitlag effect pause/resume callbacks, when translating action enter and state-transition code.
Raw literal pools adjacent to fighter action asm can be improved with `.float` and `.double` directives; typed literals can make matching and later C constant identification easier.
If exact stack layout is not yet understood, guarded dummy stack arrays can document the issue while allowing a readable C path.
naming_conventions: `ftCa_SpecialLw_*` and `ftCa_SpecialAirLw_*` distinguish grounded and aerial Falcon Kick states.
`Enter`, `Anim`, `Phys`, and `Coll` suffixes follow fighter action callback roles.
`End`, `EndAir`, and `AirLwEndAir` suffixes represent the landing/ending variants in the SpecialLw action family.
Captain character attributes use `speciallw_` prefixes for Falcon Kick-specific data fields.
Unknown fields were left with conservative names such as `speciallw_unk1`, `speciallw_unk2`, `x0`, and `x2` rather than overinterpreting behavior.
Motion vars were nested as `fp->mv.ca.speciallw`, matching the character/action-specific union organization.
Synthetic labels in inline asm were converted from `.L_800...` style to `lbl_800...` labels in the C inline-asm context.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` to select inline asm implementations for functions whose C forms did not yet match exactly.
Used `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO` plus `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop` around MWERKS inline asm for `ftCa_SpecialHi_800E3EAC`.
Preserved `nofralloc` inline asm bodies for match-sensitive functions.
Added guarded unused stack arrays such as `u8 _[8]` and `u8 _[32]` to influence stack layout in candidate C functions.
Replaced raw `.4byte` float/double encodings with `.float` and `.double` directives in nearby asm data.
Used asm-differ score reporting to document partial match percentages per function, with scores ranging approximately from 91.28% to 98.78% in the excerpt.
Kept a portable/non-MWERKS `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stub for an inline-asm-only helper instead of pretending it was decompiled.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes are available; conclusions are based on the title, file list, PR score report, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `ftCa_SpecialLw.c` functions left behind as inline asm in PR 826.
Search for later renames of `speciallw_unk1`, `speciallw_unk2`, `ftCaptainSpecialLwVars.x0`, or `x2`.
Check whether `ftCa_SpecialHi_800E3EAC` was later converted from MWERKS inline asm to C.
Look for subsequent changes to `tools/convert_data.py` after the temporary `**/ftCaptain/*.s` input glob.
Compare later Captain attribute layouts against the offset comments introduced in `ftCaptain/types.h`.
